mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate exceeding 5.5 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 55% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates synchronized demand growth that boosts corporate earnings forecasts, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the industrial production index during late-cycle expansions indicate capacity constraints and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails to predict negative returns during late-cycle phases or produces positive alpha in early-cycle recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates above 4.0 percent impose financing costs that suppress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in over 60% of high-rate observations or the signal decays to zero predictive power within 3 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 20-day returns in more than half of observations or the interaction effect reverses sign during economic contractions.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect improved household balance sheets and construction sector activity, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in over 55% of periods following housing starts increases or the correlation coefficient drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production levels above 105 signal robust manufacturing output that accelerates during post-shock recovery phases, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day returns during recovery regimes or loses predictive accuracy when industrial production falls below 100.
